subject: English
overall_learning_objectives: Understand the theme and message of the poem 'Harvest Hymn'
Identify the role of natural forces like the sun, rain, and earth in agriculture
Interpret the symbolic meaning of Surya, Varuna, and Prithvi in the poem
Recognize poetic devices such as imagery, repetition, and personification
Analyze how the poem expresses gratitude and unity during harvest time
Develop reading comprehension, interpretation, and vocabulary skills

lesson_plan.title: Understanding Harvest and Hymns
topics_covered: Meaning and purpose of a hymn
teaching_strategy: Teacher explanation of the concept of a hymn
student_activities: Discuss harvest festivals celebrated in their region
Share how these festivals express gratitude for crops

lesson_plan.title: Introduction to the Poem and Poet
topics_covered: Context and theme of the poem
Archaic words used in the poem
student_activities: Match archaic words with their meanings
Read the poem aloud in class

lesson_plan.title: Men’s Voices – Praise to Natural Forces
topics_covered: Surya as the source of sunlight
Varuna as the source of rain
Importance of sunlight and rain in farming
teaching_strategy: Discussion on how sunlight and rain affect agriculture
student_activities: Identify lines that praise Surya and Varuna
Explain how these natural forces support farming

lesson_plan.title: Women’s Voices – Earth as Mother
student_activities: Find examples of personification in the poem
Discuss why Earth is called a nurturing mother

lesson_plan.title: All Voices – Symbolism and Theme
topics_covered: Central theme of gratitude and harmony
teaching_strategy: Discussion of the poem’s central message
student_activities: Interpret the meaning of 'Seed and the Scythe'
Write a short paragraph about the poem’s message

lesson_plan.title: Language and Literary Devices
topics_covered: Poetic devices used in the poem
teaching_strategy: Teacher explanation of imagery and repetition
student_activities: Identify poetic devices from given lines

teacher_tip: Encourage students to analyze the symbolism in the poem and connect it with real-life farming practices. This helps them understand the deeper message of gratitude towards nature.
